summary: Added MAILCHIMP_API_KEY env var support with runtime datacenter parsing in internal/config/config.go, plus matching user-facing messaging in auth.go and doctor.go.
reason: Mailchimp's API key format is '{32-hex}-{dc}' where the trailing token after the last dash is the datacenter code (us1-us21). The base URL is dynamic per key: 'https://{dc}.api.mailchimp.com/3.0/'. The generator-emitted basicAuth wiring assumed two separate env vars (MAILCHIMP_USERNAME, MAILCHIMP_PASSWORD) and a fixed base URL, which doesn't match how Mailchimp users provision keys. The patch (a) reads MAILCHIMP_API_KEY as the single canonical env var, (b) parses the trailing -dc suffix via a new ParseDatacenter helper, (c) sets username='anystring' and password=key (Mailchimp's HTTP Basic convention), and (d) rewrites BaseURL to the correct datacenter host. Legacy MAILCHIMP_USERNAME/MAILCHIMP_PASSWORD still work for explicit Basic-auth users. MAILCHIMP_DC overrides the suffix for OAuth tokens with no embedded dc.
validated_outcome: doctor --json reports auth_source=env:MAILCHIMP_API_KEY, base_url=https://us6.api.mailchimp.com/3.0, api=reachable. Live ping returned 200 'Everything's Chimpy!'. Subscribe + archive + permanent-delete round-trip verified end-to-end against a real us6 account. ParseDatacenter has 11 table-driven test cases covering valid (us6/us21/us1), missing suffix, empty dc, oversized dc, non-alphanumeric dc, uppercase rejection, and empty input.
